State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive-Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                322-5901            322-6441            322-6444


December 15, 1977
77- 12-084

Robert L. Leslie
496 Alpine Lane
Sonora, California 95370

Dear Mr. Leslie:

     This is in response to your letter to me dated November 22, 1977, requesting advice with respect to your obligations under several conflict of interest laws. Specifically, you ask whether there is any conflict of interest raised by your entering into a contract with the City to place a fire hydrant on your property while you are a City Councilman in Sonora.

     Without leading you through a lengthy analysis of the Political Reform Act of 1974, the simple answer to your question is that nothing in the Act (Government Code Section 87100, et. seq.) prohibits you from entering into the contract in question.

     I have also researched Government Code Section 1090 concerning your question.1/  That section provides that:

     ...City officers...shall not be financially inter-
     ested in any contract made by them in their official
     capacity or by any body or board of which they are
     members...

     However, Government Code Section 1091.5 in amplifying on this provision states:

     (1) An officer or employee shall not be deemed to be interested in a contract if his interest is:
     ...
     (c) That of a recipient of public services generally provided by the public body or board of which he is a member on the same terms and conditions as if he were not a member of the board.


1/ The Commission and its staff are without authority to officially interpret Section 1090. Such an interpretation must come from the Office of the Attorney General. However, I hope the comments are helpful.

Robert L. Leslie
December 15, 1977
Page Two


     This section would appear to me to resolve any concerns you
may have about the fire hydrant.  Thank you for writing.  I trust
this answers your question.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth W. Goshorn
                                        Research Specialist III
                                        Conflicts of Interest Division

KWG:ma

November 22, 1977
490 Alpine Lane
Sonora, Ca., 95370


Mr. Kenneth W. Goshorn
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Division
1140 "K" Street Bldg.
Box 897
Sacramento, Ca., 95814

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

I am a Sonora City Councilman who was in to see you in August 1977, regarding a question on conflict of interest laws as they apply to me. In a copy of a contract for Fire Hydrants with the City of Sonora. The question is, "Can I enter into such an agreement for a fire hydrant on my own personal private land within Sonora during the period that I am also serving as a Sonora City Councilman?"

I would very much appreciate an answer back from you as soon as possible. Thank you.

Sincerely,

Robert L Leslie
Robert L. Leslie

City of Sonoma
...in, Robert I. Lyili

[illegible text at top]

services, therefore it is mutually

1) City shall get specifications and shall advise
requirements for such work to be
located at 277 Snell Street

2) City shall [illegible] of materials
and shall instruct Applicant(s) at any request of Owner pursuant
to City design.

2) Owners shall purchase and install hydrant(s)
pursuant to City design.

3) In the event that City installs the hydrant(s),
Owners agree to pay to City an amount equal to the actual costs
of the materials used in the installation and an amount equal to
the actual costs of labor paid from said installation.

4) City shall test and maintain hydrant(s) on a regular
basis. Owners agree to pay for the cost of upkeep on the hydrant(s).
All payments, for both installation and upkeep shall be due and
payable in advance. City shall furnish an itemized statement to
Owners showing all charges.

5) Owners agree that all liability from hydrant(s) or
the use thereof shall rest with Owners, and that City shall not
be responsible in any way for any claims resulting from the existence
or operation of the hydrant(s).

6) In the event that the property on which the hydrant(s)
are located shall be deeded or in otherwise become part of City
lands, Owner's responsibility for upkeep shall be terminated as
of the date property shall be owned by City. City, however, shall
make no payment to Owners as reimbursement for the costs of
installation.

7) This contract shall be deemed to run with the lands
associated with the hydrant(s). Accordingly, it shall be held to
be automatically assigned to the successors in interest to Owners.

In Witness Whereof, this contract was executed by:

acting on behalf of City, pursuant
to authorization of the City Council.

_________________________________    _________________________________
                                     Owners

The above was executed on _________________ in Sonoma, California.

November 22, 1977
496 Alpine Lane
Sonora, Ca., 95370


Mr. Kenneth W. Goshorn
Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Division
1100 "K" Street Bldg.
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca., 95814

Dear Mr. Goshorn:

I am the Sonora City Councilman who was in to see you in August 1977.

I have a question on conflict of interest laws as they apply to me. Enclosed is a copy of a "Contract For The Placement of Fire Hydrant" with the City of Sonora. The question is, "Can I enter into such an agreement for a fire hydrant on my own personal private land within Sonora during the period that I am also serving as a Sonora City Councilman?"

I would very much appreciate an answer back from you as soon as possible. Thank you.


Sincerely,

Robert L Leslie
Robert L. Leslie

CONDITION FOR FIRE SUPPRESSION OF LANDS WHEREBY

This Contract made and entered into by and between the
City of Sonora, a municipal corporation, hereinafter called City
and Robert L. Leslie                    hereinafter called Owners:

Whereas the Owners and nearby community desire the
installation of fire hydrant(s) on the property of Owners because
the City has the responsibility for providing fire suppression
services, therefore it is agreed as follows:

1) City shall set specifications and shall design
requirements for the installation of _1_ hydrant(s) to be
located AT 277 Shell Street                           .

2) City shall make purchases of materials
and shall install hydrants as on the request of Owners pursuant
to City design.        Or

2) Owners shall purchase and install hydrant(s)
pursuant to City design.

3) In the event that City installs the hydrant(s),
Owners agree to pay to City an amount equal to the actual costs
of the materials used in the installation and an amount equal to
the actual costs of the wages paid by those who made the installation.

4) City shall test and maintain hydrant(s) on a regular
basis. Owners agree to pay for the cost of upkeep on the hydrant(s).
All payments, for both installation and upkeep shall be due and
payable in advance. City shall furnish an itemized statement to
Owners showing all charges.

5) Owners agree that all liability from hydrant(s) or
the use thereof shall rest with Owners, and that City shall not
be responsible in any way for any claims resulting from the existance
or operation of the hydrant(s).

6) In the event that the property on which the hydrant(s)
are located shall be deeded or in otherways become part of City
lands, Owner's responsibility for upkeep shall be terminated as
of the date property shall be owned by City. City, however, shall
make no payment to Owners as reimbursement for the costs of
installation.

7) This contract shall be deemed to run with the lands
associated with the hydrant(s). Accordingly, it shall be held to
be automatically assigned to the successors in interest to Owners.

In Witness Whereof, this contract was executed by:

_________________________________    acting on behalf of City, pursuant
                                    to authorization of the City Council.


_________________________________    _________________________________
                                    Owners

The above was executed on _________    in Sonora, California.
